title: Physostegia correllii
intro: Physostegia correllii, commonly known as Texas snakeroot, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Physostegia correllii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infused oils and salves to treat various health conditions.
